items.title: Dark matter density profiles of the Milky Way satellite population: reconciling simulations and observations
items.summary: arXiv:2607.08818v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The cusp-core problem remains a key challenge for the $\Lambda$CDM model. Historically, comparing inner dark matter halo slopes ($d\log\rho/d\log r$) from simulations and observations has suffered from a methodological mismatch: eva…

items.title: What becomes of JWST/NIRCam-selected high-redshift massive galaxies?
items.summary: arXiv:2607.08820v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Early JWST/NIRCam surveys revealed a puzzling population of high-redshift massive galaxy candidates largely absent from previous rest-frame UV surveys. Spectroscopic follow-up has both confirmed and contested these candidates, whose…

items.title: The type Ia supernova 2023vjh: a peculiar 1991bg-like SN with unusually faint light curves
items.summary: arXiv:2607.08821v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present observations of the 1991bg-like type Ia supernova (SN) 2023vjh, associated with the elliptical galaxy MCG+04-10-013. Its light-curve shape parameters ($\Delta m_{15}(B)=1.89 \pm 0.01$ mag and $s_{BV} = 0.45 \pm 0.03$), to…

items.title: Star-Disk Collisions II: Debris Stream Dynamics and Implications for QPEs and Other Transients Near SMBHs
items.summary: arXiv:2607.08823v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) are repeating soft X-ray nuclear transients with recurrence times of hours-days and flare duty cycles of $\sim$10-20%. Many aspects of QPEs can be modeled as a stellar-mass orbiter that intersects an…
